ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Invalidate Trust Amendment For Lack Of Capacity And Imposition Of Constructive Trust (Filed In Consolidated Case Ptr-19-303384 On Dec. 20, 2019)
Off Calendar Nov-21-2022 Continued To Jun-12-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Guadalupe Gamino, via Zoom, for Peter Alexander and Bryanna Nicole Whitcomb, Petitioners; Lynn Searle, via Zoom, for Kenneth Whitcomb, Respondent.

The court confirms that it is inclined to take the Status of Motion to Dismiss, Status re Responsive Pleading, and Status re Evidentiary Hearing off calendar for lack of action thereon. No objections to the court's tentative ruling are presented. Counsel reports on the status of discovery and that both sides should have discovery completed by the end of January 2023. The court advises that it is inclined to set a trial date. The court mentions the pending laches issue and reminds Ms. Searle that a response to the third amended complaint is pending. Ms. Searle reports that she can have her reply filed by this week. The court reminds Mr. Gamino of the issue with the caption of his third amended complaint which still refers to the invalidation of the trust amendment for lack of capacity. After discussion regarding the preceding, the court offers Mr. Gamino the suggestion to file a corrected version of his third amended petition. Mr. Gamino advises of his plan to file the corrected petition specifically to correct the caption. Ms. Searle reports of her plan to file her response to the third amended petition as soon as it is formally filed. There is brief discussion regarding Ms. Searle's laches defense and of service regarding the 850 petition. The parties agree to set this matter for trial and counsel agrees to a trial date. The matter is set for trial in the Department of the Presiding Judge (206) on Jun-12-2023 at 9:30 a.m. Order for the same signed in open court. Counsel will be provided a copy of the order via email.

After additional discussion, the remaining Status' will be taken off calendar as stated above. The court instructs Mr. Gamino to file his corrected third amended petition on or by Nov-30-2022. Ms. Searle is instructed to file her response to the preceding by Dec-07-2022.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Invalidate Trust Amendment For Lack Of Capacity And Imposition Of Constructive Trust (Filed In Consolidated Case Ptr-19-303384 On Dec. 20, 2019)
Off Calendar Aug-22-2022 Continued To Nov-21-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Guadalupe Gamino, via Zoom, for Bryanna Nicole Whitcomb and Peter A. Whitcomb, Petitioners; Lynn Searle, via Zoom, for Kenneth Whitcomb, Respondent.

The court acknowledges the third amended petition that is pending in the court's e-filing queue. The court confirms with Ms. Searle that her motion to dismiss is pending until additional discovery is propounded and agrees that the laches issue should be bifurcated and set for an evidentiary hearing. After additional discussion on the status of this matter, Ms. Searle advises that she expects to file a response to the third amended petition.

All subject matters are continued to Nov-21-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A Status Re: Discovery, A Status Re: A Responsive Pleading, and A Status Re: An Evidentiary Hearing, are set for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Invalidate Trust Amendment For Lack Of Capacity And Imposition Of Constructive Trust (Filed In Consolidated Case Ptr-19-303384 On Dec. 20, 2019)
Off Calendar Jul-11-2022 Continued To Aug-22-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Guadalupe Gamino, via Zoom, for Peter Whitcomb and Bryanna Whitcomb, Petitioners; Lynn Searle, via Zoom, for Kenneth Whitcomb, Respondent. The court and counsel discuss the unsigned copy of the trust and whether counsel has discussed possibly bifurcation. Ms. Searle reports of possibly bifurcating and mentions laches as grounds for a motion to dismiss the quiet title portion of the second amended petition. The court points to the strong arguments submitted by Ms. Searle regarding the demurrer issue. Mr. Gamino reports that he is willing to drop the quiet title action and offers testimony regarding his position regarding the title. The court queries Mr. Gamino regarding the status of his 3rd amended petition. Mr. Gamino reports of sending Ms. Searle a copy recently but would like to resend a different version which would not include a request regarding cancellation or quiet title. The court instructs Mr. Gamino to make the alterations he mentions and forward to Ms. Searle. The court confirms with Ms. Searle that she can have a motion drafted within the next three to four weeks.

The Case Management Hearing and Status on Meet and Confer are taken off calendar. The Petition to Invalidate, etc., is continued to Aug-22-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A Status on Motion to Dismiss and a Status of Third Amended Petition are set for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Saephan. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing.
Off Calendar Jul-11-2022 Text Ruling

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Invalidate Trust Amendment For Lack Of Capacity And Imposition Of Constructive Trust (Filed In Consolidated Case Ptr-19-303384 On Dec. 20, 2019)
Off Calendar May-09-2022 Continued To Jul-11-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Guadalupe Gamino, via Zoom, for Peter and Brianna Whitcomb, Petitioners; Lynn Searle, for Kenneth Whitcomb, Respondent.

Mr. Gamino reports that notice regarding the second amended petition has not been provided and is considering filing a third amendment and offers testimony regarding his plans to do so. Ms. Searle reports of a meet and confer and discussed claim of title and offers the court her current position and that she would stipulate to the filing of the third amended petition. The court points to its frustration with this matter and to the fact that they did not file an updated joint case management statement. The court strongly urges counsel to continue to meet and confer.

After additional discussion, the matters set for hearing today are continued to Jul-11-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. Counsel is instructed to file a joint case management statement including status of the following: 1) meet and confer, 2) the 3rd Amended Petition, and 3) a demurrer, on or before Jun-27-2022. Mr. Gamino confirms that he will be preparing the joint case management statement.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Invalidate Trust Amendment For Lack Of Capacity And Imposition Of Constructive Trust (Filed In Consolidated Case Ptr-19-303384 On Dec. 20, 2019)
Off Calendar Apr-11-2022 Continued To May-09-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lynn Searle, via Zoom, for Kenneth Whitcomb, Respondent; Guadalupe Gamino, via Zoom, for Peter and Bryanna Whitcomb, Petitioners.

The court reminds Mr. Gamino that notice of the amended petition has yet to be filed with the court. Ms. Searle offers testimony regarding her failure to appear at the previous calling of this matter. Ms. Searle reports on the dispute regarding the 2007 amendment and that there is evidence of a signed copy of the amendment but that it cannot be located and issues locating and confirming records regarding the amendment. Ms. Searle reports that she has issues with some of the causes of action and that Mr. Gamino has refused to meet and confer and plans to file a demurrer accordingly. Mr. Gamino advises that he doesn't believe that at this point a meet and confer will not be productive and amicably lead to a resolution. The court advises Mr. Gamino that a meet and confer might be more efficient than going through the demurrer process.

After additional discussion, the matters are continued to May-09-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A Status on Meet and Confer or Filing a Demurrer are set for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Gomez. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Invalidate Trust Amendment For Lack Of Capacity And Imposition Of Constructive Trust (Filed In Consolidated Case Ptr-19-303384 On Dec. 20, 2019)
Off Calendar Feb-07-2022 Continued To Apr-11-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Guadalupe Gamino appeared by zoom for Petitioners.

The court noted that Ms. Searle was not present. The matter was passed so that the clerk and Mr. Gamino could make attempts to contact Ms. Searle. The matter was recalled. Both the clerk and Mr. Gamino were unsuccessful in reaching her. Mr. Gamino stated that it was unusual for her not to appear and that he would not mind continuing the matter. The Amended Petition to Invalidate Trust Amendment, etc. and the case management hearing are continued to April 11, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The court encouraged Mr. Gamino to get his amended petition on file.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: G. Bastain. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion To Amend Petition To Invalid Trsut Amendment
Off Calendar Jan-26-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Invalidate Trust Amendment For Lack Of Capacity And Imposition Of Constructive Trust (Filed In Consolidated Case Ptr-19-303384 On Dec. 20, 2019)
Off Calendar Dec-13-2021 Continued To Feb-07-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Guadalupe Gamino, via ZOOM, for Peter J. Whitcomb, Jr., Peter Alexander Whitcomb, and Bryanna Whitcomb, Petitioners; Lynne Searle, via ZOOM, for Kenneth Whitcomb, Trustee.

The court begins by advising counsel that it is considering ordering sanctions in the amount of $500 for failure to comply with the court's previous order to file a joint case management statement. Ms. Searle and Mr. Gamino provide testimony regarding what caused the failure to jointly file a case management statement. The court reads the Case Management order in open court pointing specifically to the court's directives shown on page four of the order. The court then queries Ms. Searle regarding whether she is considering objecting to Mr. Gamino's motion to amend the petition. Ms. Searle reports that she had discussed the motion to amend with Mr. Gamino and is not certain that she will oppose the motion. The court advises that typically, amendments to petitions are rarely denied and suggests that the parties agree and stipulate to the amendment to save resources. After additional discussion, the court advises the parties that the matters on calendar today are being continued and instructs Mr. Gamino to draft a joint case management statement, in compliance with the court's order dated Nov-02-2021. The court instructs counsel to meet and confer regarding the motion to amend scheduled for Jan-26-2022.

The Amended Petition to Invalidate, etc., and Case Management Hearing are continued to Feb-07-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. Mr. Gamino is instructed to draft a Joint Case Management Statement and send it to Ms. Searle by Jan-19-2022. The parties are instructed to file the Joint Case Management Statement by Jan-28-2022.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing.
Off Calendar Dec-13-2021 Continued To Feb-07-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Invalidate Trust Amendment For Lack Of Capacity And Imposition Of Constructive Trust (Filed In Consolidated Case Ptr-19-303384 On Dec. 20, 2019)
Off Calendar Oct-25-2021 Continued To Dec-13-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lynn Searle, via ZOOM, for Kenneth Whitcomb, Respondent; Guadalupe Gamino, Peter Whitcomb, Jr., Peter A. Whitcomb, and Bryanna N. Whitcomb.

The court confirms with Mr. Gamino that Peter Whitcomb, Jr., is no longer challenging the 2003 amendment and that he plans to file an amended petition excluding Peter Whitcomb, Jr. as a party. The court then asks if the original signed copy of the 2007 amendment has been found. Ms. Searle reports that it has not and is uncertain if it will be located. Ms. Searle reports that she and Mr. Gamino have met and conferred regarding settlement discussions but that she is waiting for a copy of the amended petition from Mr. Gamino which would make for more meaningful settlement discussions. Ms. Searle offers oral testimony regarding the issues that have caused delays in resolving this matter including that discovery has yet to be finalized. Mr. Gamino advises that the only discovery that remains for him is a deposition of Ms. Searles' client but that the pandemic had caused delays in calendaring the deposition. Mr. Gamino reports that his amended petition should be ready soon, to be brought under Probate Code 855, and hopes to have a copy of the petition to Ms. Seale by this Friday. Ms. Searle advises that she doesn't believe that settlement discussions would be helpful until discovery has been completed and she has had a chance to review Mr. Gamino's amended petition. The court advises Mr. Gamino that the ball is in his court now and that he does not need to wait for Ms. Searle to review his amended petition before filing it. Mr. Gamino reports that he wanted to send the amended petition to Ms. Searle first to see if she'd be amenable to him filing it to avoid having to petition the court for an order to file the amended petition. The court advises the parties that it will be issuing a case management order.

The Amended Petition to Invalidate Trust, etc., is continued to Dec-13-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204. The parties are instructed to file a joint case management statement by Dec-03-2021.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Invalidate Trust Amendment For Lack Of Capacity And Imposition Of Constructive Trust (Filed In Consolidated Case Ptr-19-303384 On Dec. 20, 2019)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Invalidate Trust Amendment For Lack Of Capacity And Imposition Of Constructive Trust (Filed In Consolidated Case Ptr-19-303384 On Dec. 20, 2019)
Off Calendar Jul-21-2021 Continued To Sep-15-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Guadalupe Gamino, via ZOOM, for Peter Whitcomb, Jr., Peter Alexander Whitcomb, and Bryanna Whitcomb, Petitioners; Lynn Searle, via ZOOM, for Kenneth Whitcomb, Respondent.

The court begins by asking Ms. Searle if she has received notice of the amended petition. Ms. Searle reports that she did receive a copy via File and Serve Express but could not confirm whether it has been filed by the court. The court then asks Ms. Searle if she anticipates filing an objection, Ms. Searle confirms that she does intend to. Counsel provides oral testimony after which the court queries counsel regarding any ongoing settlement discussions. Mr. Gamino reports that there have not but that he would be amenable to doing so. The court then queries counsel whether they may be amenable to participating in the court's pro bono mediation program. Ms. Searle believes that the petitioners do not have a solid case and the allegations are not supportable but will discuss the prospect of settlement discussions with her client. Ms. Searle also reports that her forthcoming objection will include her roadmap for this case.

The Petition to Invalidate Trust Amendment, etc., is continued to September 15, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Invalidate Trust Amendment For Lack Of Capacity And Imposition Of Constructive Trust (Filed In Consolidated Case Ptr-19-303384 On Dec. 20, 2019)
Off Calendar Jun-09-2021 Continued To Jul-21-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Guadalupe Gamino, via ZOOM, counsel for Peter Whitcomb, Jr., Petitioner; Lynn Searle, via ZOOM, counsel for Kenneth Whitcomb, Trustee.

The court begins by focusing on Mr. Gamino's lack of progress with this matter. Mr. Gamino reports that he just recently electronically filed notice of hearing on the amended petition. Ms. Searle reports that discovery remains the primary issue as well as not receiving timely notice and that Mr. Gamino has not designated her clients as a party on File and Serve Express. After additional discussion, the court advises Mr. Gamino to make certain to provide courtesy copies to the Examiner assigned to this matter.

The Petition to Invalidate Trust Amendment, etc., is continued to July 21, 2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
